channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Spain’s De la Fuente becomes oldest coach to win World Cup
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spain’s Luis de la Fuente, 65, made history as the oldest coach to win the FIFA World Cup, surpassing Vicente del Bosque’s record. Learn more about his tri
Read More: https://punchng.com/spains-de-la-fuente-becomes-oldest-coach-to-win-world-cup/

item.title: NDIC begins payment to depositors of 46 failed microfinance banks
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NDIC has commenced payment of insured deposits to customers of 46 failed microfinance banks. Learn how to claim your NDIC payment.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ndic-begins-payment-to-depositors-of-46-failed-microfinance-banks/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Apologise and resign, Atiku tells Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Atiku Abubakar calls for President Tinubu’s apology and resignation over the PFIPC scandal, citing conflicting accounts from the AGF and CBN.
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-apologise-and-resign-atiku-tells-tinubu/

item.title: Senate seeks tougher mining safety rules after Kogi landslide kills 20
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Following a tragic landslide in Kogi that killed 20, the Senate is pushing for tougher mining safety rules and a workers’ compensation scheme in Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-seeks-tougher-mining-safety-rules-after-kogi-landslide-kills-20/

item.description:

item.title: If you pay a hacker’s ransom, chances are that they’ll come back for more
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The latest research reaffirmed that hackers often demanded more money if a victim paid the first time.

item.description: The ruling party also launched a personal attack on the former vice president, alleging that his criticisms were influenced by what it described as "the ghost of his sordid past."

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Debt Management Office (DMO) has raised a total of N929.32 billion at its July 2026 Federal Government bond auction, less than N1.2 trillion it planned to raise, despite strong investor demand for the three reopened instruments offered for subscription. The DMO had offered N1.2 trillion, compris

item.title: 2030 World Cup: FIFA reach out to Super Eagles
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: FIFA has expressed confidence that Nigeria can qualify for the 2030 FIFA World Cup, but says the Super Eagles must maintain stability if they want to return to football’s biggest tournament.

item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky has bowed to pressure from the streets. The former head of the Ukrainian armed forces Oleksandr Syrsky has been replaced by the popular Mykhailo Drapaty. Who is he?

item.description: Kasim Sumaina in Abuja  The Federal Government on Wednesday pledged to ensure that resolutions from the 18th International Maritime Seminar for Judges are translated into policy, legislation, and institutional reforms.

item.description: The high cost of poultry feed and the large space needed to rear chickens are forcing many Nigerian farmers to look for cheaper, faster alternatives. The post Why small-scale farmers are switching to quail farming appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The humanitarian organization Médecins Sans Frontières, MSF, has raised the alarm over a worsening cholera outbreak in Borno State, reporting 35,000 suspected cases and 198 deaths so far. item.title: NPFL: Rangers sign Azeez Falolu from Katsina United
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Rangers have completed the signing of forward Azeez Falolu from Katsina United, DAILY POST reports. Falolu joined the reigning Nigeria Premier Football League, NPFL, champions following lengthy discussions between both sides. He becomes one of Rangers’ early additions of the summer transfer window.

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A father of 11, Lawal Hamisu, has asked a Sharia Court in Kaduna State to compel his 27-year-old daughter, Fatima Lawal, to get married. Hamisu made the request before Shari’a Court II sitting at Magajin Gari, Kaduna, on Tuesday through his counsel, H.B. Debo. According to the complainant, his

item.title: Kano launches task force to combat drug abuse, thuggery
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kano State Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf has launched a multi-agency task force on drug abuse and signed an executive order to tackle thuggery and other criminal activities in the state. According to a statement by the governor’s spokesperson, Sunusi Bature Dawakin Tofa, Yusuf said drug abuse remained o

item.title: Bad Loans Threaten Argentina’s Next Engine of Economic Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bad loans keep piling up in Argentina, dashing hopes that the economic pain unleashed by President Javier Milei’s harsh austerity program had already crested.

item.title: Parallel market trap: Why hoarding dollars in July is massive gamble
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: For many Nigerians, buying and holding United States dollars has long been seen as a way to protect wealth whenever the naira comes under pressure. The post Parallel market trap: Why hoarding dollars in July is massive gamble appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Island, Says War Has Cost $37.5 Billion
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The US military said on Tuesday it had launched an eleventh consecutive night of strikes against Iran, saying it targeted military sites to "further degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz". The post US Strikes Iranian Island, Says War Has Cost $37.5 Billion ap

item.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to Anthropic
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: AMD says it's going to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, while helping to expand the AI company's computing power, according to an announcement on Wednesday. As part of the new partnership, Anthropic will deploy up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's Instinct MI450 AI GPUs using the chipmaker's new Helios r

item.title: 'Unprecedented': France records 5,764 excess deaths during June-July heatwave period
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: France recorded 5,764 more deaths than the normal rate between June 17 and July 2, health authorities said Wednesday. While authorities said the excess mortality rate was due to "all causes", the period coincided with a stretch of extreme heatwaves in several French regions.

item.title: Operation HADIN KAI Eliminates ISWAP Chief Photographer, Others
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Operation HADIN KAI (OPHK) has confirmed the elimination of Abu Salim al-Barnawi, a notorious Boko Haram/ISWAP raid commander turned chief photographer and propaganda operative, during the failed terrorist attack on a military location last week. The post Operation HADIN KAI Eliminates ISWAP Chief P

item.title: Insecurity: Reps Demand Alternative Road To Abuja From Keffi-Karu Axis
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives on Wednesday, urged the Federal Ministry of Works, in collaboration with the Federal Capital Territory Administration (FCTA), to undertake a comprehensive technical and engineering feasibility study on the development of an alternative access road linking the Keffi-Karu

item.title: Tankers make sharp U-turns after Houthi shipping threat
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: All of the ships were travelling to or from Saudi ports before changing course, ship-tracking data shows.

item.title: 'Silly Mr Bodybuilder!' - Fury casts doubt over Joshua fight
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Tyson Fury says he is prepared to "fight somebody else" at the end of 2026 if his long-awaited showdown with Anthony Joshua does not go ahead.

item.title: ICPC Recovers N942m In Salaries Paid To 908 Ghost Workers
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has uncovered 908 ghost workers across at least 50 federal ministries and agencies, recovering approximately N942m in fraudulent salary payments made to individuals who never existed as legitimate government employees. Ac
